vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data confirms the presence of two primary containers: a yellow pot (P1) and a black pot (P3/P4). The expected P2 (Mexican Mint) is not visually distinct from the soil/debris in the black pot. The white rabbit anchor is present in the black pot.

inventory_reconciliation.New_Introductions: A metallic object (spoon) appears in the T-4 to Current sequence, likely an intervention tool.

plant_audit.P1: Soil surface appears dark and undisturbed. No visible String of Nickels foliage.
plant_audit.P3: Contains the white rabbit anchor. Soil surface shows white granular debris (likely perlite or eggshell). No healthy Pothos foliage visible.
plant_audit.P4: No evidence of Silver Guest foliage.

biome_observations.debris: Presence of white granular material in P3. A metal spoon is present in the workspace from T-4 onwards.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Introduction of a metallic spoon near the yellow pot. No change in plant vitality.
temporal_deltas.T-2: Significant drop in ambient light or camera exposure; visibility of plant structures is near zero.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_Current: Return of visibility confirms no recovery or new growth. The biome remains in a state of stasis or decline.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The specimens are in a state of critical failure. There is no visible chlorophyll-bearing tissue in any of the pots. The environment appears to be a 'dead' biome with no active plant life visible.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a spoon suggests human intervention/maintenance attempts, but the biological state remains non-viable.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a chronological audit of the provided image sequence. The biome is currently in a state of systemic failure. Despite the presence of the white rabbit anchor in P3, the associated Pothos is not visible. P1 (String of Nickels) shows no signs of life. The introduction of a spoon in the later frames indicates an attempt at soil manipulation or maintenance, but this has not resulted in any visible vegetative recovery. The plants are effectively lost.
